---
title: "Big Data & Analytics"
format: html
---

::: callout-note
Some of the following will be moved to the [syllabus](syllabus.html).
:::

This module introduces students to the methods, technologies, and governance of modern data analytics across descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive approaches. It builds a comprehensive understanding of the data analytics process—from data structuring and preparation to exploratory data analysis, analytical modeling, and decision support.
The module combines conceptual foundations and hands-on analytical practice using Python, pandas, and scikit-learn. It progresses from structured transactional data (data warehouses, OLAP, data mining) to big and unstructured data (text, social, streaming). Emphasis is placed on the fit between data types, analytical models, and decision purposes, as well as the ability to design, evaluate, and govern analytical systems in organizations.

## Learning objectives

**Knowledge** After completing the module, students will be able to:

- **Differentiate** between descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics approaches. 🟢 [l1](slides/lecture_1_foundations_of_data_analytics.html)
- **Describe** architectures for data warehousing and big data processing, with reference to the underlying data modeling concepts. 🟢 [l2](slides/lecture_2_descriptive_analytics.html)
- **Explain** fundamental concepts of data analytics, and its role in data-driven organizations. 🟢 [l10](slides/lecture_10_data_governance.html)

**Skills** Students will be able to:

- **Select** appropriate analytical methods for transactional or non-transactional data, and for descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analysis tasks.
- **Apply** analytical methods to real-world datasets, including structuring, transforming, and visualizing data. This also involves training models, evaluating performance, and interpreting analytical results for decision-making.
- **Implement** analytical procedures in Python, using standard data science libraries (pandas, scikit-learn, matplotlib).

**Competence** Upon successful completion, students will be able to:

- **Design** analytics solutions aligned with business goals and governance principles.
- **Integrate** analytical technologies into organizational decision-making processes
- **Assess** the operational and strategic implications of data analytics in organizations. 🟢 [l10](slides/lecture_10_data_governance)

## Lectures and exercises

| Date  | Part | Session | Topic | Materials |
|------|------|---------|-------|-----------|
|  | – | – | Course Logistics | [slides](slides/lecture_0_orga.html), [notes](notes/lecture_0_orga.html) |
| 2026-03-23 | Foundations | Session 1 | Foundations of data analytics | [slides](slides/lecture_1_foundations_of_data_analytics.html), [notes](notes/lecture_1_foundations_of_data_analytics.html) |
| 2026-03-23 | Structured Data | Session 2 | Structuring and preparing data | [slides](slides/lecture_2_structuring_preparing_data.html), [notes](notes/lecture_2_structuring_preparing_data.html) |
| 2026-03-30 | Structured Data | Session 3 | Descriptive analysis I | [slides](slides/lecture_3_descriptive_analysis_1.html), [notes](notes/lecture_3_descriptive_analysis_1.html) |
| 2026-03-30 | Structured Data | Session 4 | Descriptive analysis II | [slides](slides/lecture_4_descriptive_analysis_2.html), [notes](notes/lecture_4_descriptive_analysis_2.html) |
| | | Break | | |
| 2026-04-14 | Structured Data | Session 5 | Predictive analysis | [slides](slides/lecture_5_predictive_analysis.html), [notes](notes/lecture_5_predictive_analysis.html) |
| 2026-04-20 | Structured Data | Session 6 | Prescriptive analysis | [slides](slides/lecture_6_prescriptive_analysis.html), [notes](notes/lecture_6_prescriptive_analysis.html) |
| 2026-04-20 | Big Data | Session 7 | Big data | [slides](slides/lecture_7_big_data.html), [notes](notes/lecture_7_big_data.html) |
| | | Break | | |
| 2026-04-04 | Big Data | Session 8 | Exploratory analysis | [slides](slides/lecture_8_exploratory_analysis.html), [notes](notes/lecture_8_exploratory_analysis.html) |
| 2026-04-04 | Big Data | Session 9 | Predictive analysis | [slides](slides/lecture_9_predictive_analysis.html), [notes](notes/lecture_9_predictive_analysis.html) |
| 2026-04-11 | Organizations | Session 10 | Governance of data analytics | [slides](slides/lecture_10_data_governance.html), [notes](notes/lecture_10_data_governance.html) |
| 2026-04-11 | Organizations | Session 11 | Recap, exam preparation, Q&A | [slides](slides/lecture_11_recap.html), [notes](notes/lecture_11_recap.html) |

<!--
### Part 1: Foundations
### Part 2: Analytics for structured data
### Part 3: Analytics for big data
### Part 4 – Analytics in organizations
-->

::: callout-note
We may prepare the [handout](all_lectures.pdf) generated based on all slides.
:::

## Group work

-  To come

## Teaching notes

- To come (adding link to confidential exam repository)

## License, code of conduct, contributing, ...

## Recommended literature


Part 1:

- Sharda, R., Delen, D., & Turban, E. (2018). Business intelligence, analytics, and data science: a managerial perspective. [link](https://elibrary.pearson.de/book/99.150005/9781292727530)
- Martínez-Plumed, F., Contreras-Ochando, L., Ferri, C., Hernández-Orallo, J., Kull, M., Lachiche, N., Ramirez-Quintana, M. J. & Flach, P. (2019). CRISP-DM twenty years later: From data mining processes to data science trajectories. _IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering_, _33_(8), 3048-3061. [link](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/8943998)
- Chen, H., Chiang, R. H., & Storey, V. C. (2012). Business intelligence and analytics: From big data to big impact. _MIS Quarterly_ 36(4), 1165-1188. [link](https://www.jstor.org/stable/41703503)

Part 2:

- Vaisman, A.; Zimányi, E. (2016): Data Warehouse Systems: Design and Implementation (Data-Centric Systems and Applications), Springer. [link](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-662-65167-4)
- Schutt, R.; O’Neil, C. (2014): Doing Data Science, O’Reilly Media. [link](https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/doing-data-science/9781449363871/)
- Raschka, Sebastian (2015): Python Machine Learning, Packt Publishing. [link](https://www.packtpub.com/en-us/product/python-machine-learning-9781783555130)

Part 3:

- Schmarzo, B. (2016): Big Data MBA, Wiley. [link](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/book/10.1002/9781119238881)
- Bengfort, B., Bilbro, R., & Ojeda, T. (2018). _Applied text analysis with python: Enabling language-aware data products with machine learning_. O'Reilly Media, Inc. [link](https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/applied-text-analysis/9781491963036/)

Part 4:

- Davenport, T. H. (2006). Competing on analytics. _Harvard Business Review_, _84_(1), 98. [link](https://cs.brown.edu/courses/cs295-11/competing.pdf)
- Vidgen, R., Shaw, S., & Grant, D. B. (2017). Management challenges in creating value from business analytics. _European Journal of Operational Research_, _261_(2), 626-639. [link](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0377221717301455)
